itunes_results.name: Speeko - Public Speaking Coach
itunes_results.category: speaking exercise app — short daily speaking prompts and recording; exercise drill tool not a daily public speaking science education habit with streak XP, communication mastery badges (Fear Dismantler, Vocal Authority Builder, Audience Psychology Expert), and evidence-based curriculum from communication science to stage presence neuroscience

itunes_results.name: Orai - Public Speaking App
itunes_results.category: AI feedback speaking app — analyzes filler words, pacing, energy; diagnostic tool not a daily public speaking science habit with streak XP, rhetoric mastery badges (Aristotle's Triangle Expert, Vocal Variety Engineer, Audience Emotion Architect), and progression from anxious speaker to confident science-informed communicator

itunes_results.name: Toastmasters Official
itunes_results.category: meeting management app — tracks speech evaluations and club meetings; club admin tool not a daily public speaking science education habit with streak XP, persuasion science mastery badges, and structured curriculum from the neuroscience of audience attention to advanced storytelling frameworks

daily_public_speaking_streak.notes: 2.67M public speaking community. Speeko and Orai offer drills and diagnostics. Neither teaches the science of WHY great speakers hold attention. Gap: one communication science concept per day (what the basal ganglia fear response is and why it fires during public speaking, how the three Aristotelian appeals work neurologically in modern audiences, why vocal variety activates mirror neurons more than monotone delivery, what the primacy-recency effect means for speech structure, how storytelling bypasses the prefrontal cortex's skepticism filter) with streak XP, rhetoric mastery badges (Fear Scientist, Aristotle Scholar, Neurological Storyteller), and progression from stage-fright sufferer to confident science-backed communicator. Jerry Seinfeld, Chris Anderson's TED Talks book (1M sold) — massive audience craving the science behind great speaking.

itunes_results.name: Blinkist
itunes_results.category: book summary app — 15-min summaries of nonfiction books; passive reading not a daily rhetoric science education habit with streak XP, argumentation mastery badges (Logical Fallacy Hunter, Socratic Method Expert, Ethos/Pathos/Logos Architect), and structured curriculum from classical rhetoric to modern persuasion science

itunes_results.name: Duolingo
itunes_results.category: gamified language learning app — vocabulary and grammar drills for 40 languages; language practice not a daily rhetoric and argumentation science habit with streak XP, classical persuasion mastery badges, and evidence-based curriculum from ancient Greek rhetoric to modern behavioral economics of persuasion

itunes_results.name: Khan Academy
itunes_results.category: educational platform — covers grammar and writing basics in its curriculum; general education platform not a specialized daily rhetoric science habit with streak XP, advanced argumentation mastery badges (Syllogism Builder, Fallacy Classifier, Counterargument Architect), and progressive mastery of the science and craft of persuasion

daily_rhetoric_streak.notes: 3.65M rhetoric/debate community. Blinkist summarizes rhetoric books; no app teaches the systematic science. Gap: one rhetoric concept per day (what the Toulmin model of argument is and how to apply it, how ethos is constructed through specific verbal and nonverbal signals, why the anchoring heuristic makes first numbers in negotiation so sticky, what Cialdini's six principles are and why they work neurologically, how to identify and deploy ad hominem vs. strawman vs. modus tollens distinctions) with streak XP, argumentation mastery badges (Classical Rhetorician, Fallacy Detective, Persuasion Engineer), and progression from casual debater to systematic rhetorical scientist. Cialdini's Influence sold 5M copies — proven appetite for the science of persuasion.

itunes_results.name: Drops - Language Learning
itunes_results.category: visual vocabulary app — picture-word association for 50 languages; vocabulary drilling tool not a daily linguistics science education habit with streak XP, language structure mastery badges (Phoneme Expert, Morphology Architect, Syntax Engineer), and evidence-based curriculum from how the brain acquires language to universal grammar theory

itunes_results.name: Pimsleur
itunes_results.category: audio language learning app — spaced repetition audio drills for 50 languages; audio drill system not a daily linguistics science habit with streak XP, language science mastery badges (Spaced Repetition Scientist, Critical Period Theorist, Bilingual Brain Builder), and structured curriculum from Chomsky's universal grammar to the neuroscience of how accent forms in adulthood

itunes_results.name: AnkiMobile Flashcards
itunes_results.category: spaced repetition flashcard app — custom card decks with SM-2 algorithm; study tool not a daily linguistics science education habit with streak XP, language acquisition mastery badges, and progression from flashcard user to language science practitioner who understands WHY spaced repetition works neurologically

daily_linguistics_streak.notes: 3.2M linguistics/language science community. Language learning apps (Duolingo, Drops, Pimsleur) teach vocabulary and grammar but never explain WHY language works the way it does. Gap: one linguistics science concept per day (what phonemes vs. morphemes vs. syntax are and how they layer, how Chomsky's universal grammar theory explains why all languages share deep structure, why the critical period hypothesis means accent reduction gets harder after age 12, what code-switching reveals about bilingual brain organization, how Sapir-Whorf hypothesis predicts language shapes thought) with streak XP, linguistics mastery badges (Phonologist, Morphologist, Syntactician, Pragmatician), and progression from language learner to language scientist. Linguistics is the meta-skill above every language app — 750K dedicated subreddit, 2M language learners craving the 'why'.

itunes_results.name: Merriam-Webster Dictionary
itunes_results.category: dictionary app — word definitions, thesaurus, word of the day; reference tool not a daily etymology science education habit with streak XP, word history mastery badges (Latin Root Expert, Greek Cognate Mapper, Proto-Indo-European Reconstructor), and progression from dictionary user to etymology detective who traces words to Proto-Indo-European roots

itunes_results.name: Dictionary.com
itunes_results.category: dictionary and vocabulary app — definitions, word of the day, spelling bee; reference tool not a daily etymology science habit with streak XP, word origin mastery badges, and evidence-based curriculum from how Latin and Greek roots built English vocabulary to how sound change laws (Grimm's Law) predict cognates across Germanic languages

itunes_results.name: Vocabulary.com
itunes_results.category: vocabulary learning app — adaptive quiz-based word learning with definitions; vocabulary drilling not a daily etymology science education habit with streak XP, word history mastery badges, and structured curriculum from the Norman Conquest's French impact on English to how scientific Latin naming works

daily_etymology_streak.notes: 1.7M etymology/word-history community. Dictionary apps define words but never trace their origins systematically. Gap: one etymology concept per day (where does 'salary' come from and what does Roman salt payment reveal about ancient economics, how Grimm's Law explains why English 'father' and Latin 'pater' and German 'Vater' are the same word, what the Norman Conquest of 1066 did to English vocabulary creating our formal/informal word pairs like 'begin/commence' and 'cow/beef', why medical terminology is mostly Greek and legal terminology is mostly Latin, how knowing 200 Greek and Latin roots unlocks 80% of academic English vocabulary) with streak XP, etymology mastery badges (Root Detective, Latin Scholar, Greek Cognate Mapper, Proto-Indo-European Pioneer). Secondary market: SAT/GRE vocab prep — parents and students. ICP: word nerds, writers, academics, SAT preppers.

itunes_results.name: Social Skills Training
itunes_results.category: social skills app — scenario-based social situation training; scripted scenario practice not a daily body language science education habit with streak XP, nonverbal mastery badges (Micro-Expression Scientist, Proxemics Expert, Kinesics Interpreter), and evidence-based curriculum from Paul Ekman's micro-expressions to Edward Hall's proxemics distance zones

itunes_results.name: Replika
itunes_results.category: AI companion chatbot — emotional support and conversation practice; AI chat companion not a daily body language science habit with streak XP, nonverbal communication mastery badges, and structured curriculum from the science of eye contact and pupil dilation to how posture signals dominance and submission in primate research

itunes_results.name: Woebot
itunes_results.category: CBT chatbot — cognitive behavioral therapy exercises for mental health; mental health tool not a daily body language and nonverbal science education habit with streak XP, kinesics mastery badges (Gesture Decoder, Micro-Expression Reader, Proxemics Calibrator), and progression from body-language amateur to certified nonverbal communication practitioner

daily_body_language_streak.notes: 4M social skills/behavioral science community. No app teaches the systematic science of nonverbal communication. Gap: one body language science concept per day (what Paul Ekman's seven universal micro-expressions are and how to identify contempt vs. disgust, how Edward Hall's four proxemic zones (intimate/personal/social/public) differ across cultures, why the power pose research (Amy Cuddy) affects cortisol and testosterone levels, what baseline deviation means in behavioral detection and how the FBI uses it, how mirroring activates mirror neurons and creates unconscious rapport) with streak XP, nonverbal mastery badges (Kinesics Scholar, Micro-Expression Reader, Proxemics Expert, Deception Detector), and progression from body language amateur to trained nonverbal scientist. Joe Navarro's What Every Body Is Saying (1M sold) proves the market — nobody has gamified the daily learning.

itunes_results.name: ELSA Speak
itunes_results.category: AI pronunciation coach — speech recognition scores your English pronunciation per phoneme; pronunciation feedback tool not a daily accent science education habit with streak XP, phonology mastery badges (IPA Phoneme Expert, Allophone Mapper, Coarticulation Scientist), and structured curriculum from how the International Phonetic Alphabet maps every human speech sound to the articulatory phonetics of tongue position and airflow

itunes_results.name: English Pronunciation
itunes_results.category: pronunciation practice app — British and American phonics training with audio; phonics drilling not a daily accent science education habit with streak XP, articulatory phonetics mastery badges, and evidence-based curriculum from the acoustic phonetics of vowel formants to why specific L1 speakers have specific L2 pronunciation errors

itunes_results.name: SpeechAce
itunes_results.category: AI speech scoring app — phoneme-level feedback for English pronunciation; diagnostic feedback tool not a daily accent science education habit with streak XP, phonological mastery, and progression from unaware mispronouncer to trained phonologist who understands the articulatory basis of every error

daily_accent_training_streak.notes: 2.44M accent/pronunciation community. ELSA provides feedback; nobody teaches the science of why accent exists and how to systematically change it. Gap: one articulatory phonetics concept per day (what the International Phonetic Alphabet (IPA) is and why it maps every human sound precisely, how vowel formants F1 and F2 define the acoustic 'fingerprint' of each vowel, why L1 interference happens — the native language phonological system filters new sounds, what coarticulation means and why it makes 'natural' speech hard to parse for learners, how motor theory of speech perception explains why seeing lip movement helps comprehension) with streak XP, phonology mastery badges (IPA Scholar, Vowel Space Navigator, Phoneme Engineer, Coarticulation Expert). Primary ICP: non-native English speakers seeking accent reduction — 1.5B English learners globally. Secondary: actors, voice coaches, speech therapists.

itunes_results.name: The ASL App
itunes_results.category: ASL vocabulary app — native Deaf signer video lessons for American Sign Language; vocabulary reference tool not a daily ASL linguistics science education habit with streak XP, sign language mastery badges (Handshape Expert, Non-Manual Marker Scholar, ASL Grammar Scientist), and evidence-based curriculum from how ASL grammar differs from English (spatial grammar vs. linear grammar) to the linguistics of classifier predicates

itunes_results.name: Marlee Signs
itunes_results.category: ASL learning app with Marlee Matlin — celebrity-fronted beginner ASL vocabulary; celebrity endorsement vocabulary tool not a daily ASL science education habit with streak XP, Deaf culture mastery badges, and structured curriculum from the phonological parameters of sign language (handshape, location, movement, orientation, NMM) to the sociolinguistics of Deaf community identity

itunes_results.name: ASL - American Sign Language
itunes_results.category: ASL dictionary and phrase reference — searchable sign video library; reference lookup tool not a daily ASL linguistics science education habit with streak XP, sign language science mastery badges, and progression from vocabulary lookup user to ASL linguistics scholar who understands the full grammatical system

daily_sign_language_streak.notes: 323K ASL/sign language community (underrepresented — actual interest far larger: ASL is the 3rd most studied language in US universities). ASL apps teach vocabulary signs; nobody teaches the linguistics of sign language. Gap: one ASL linguistics concept per day (what the 5 parameters of ASL signs are — handshape, location, movement, orientation, non-manual markers — and how changing one changes meaning, how ASL grammar uses spatial grammar instead of word order, what classifier predicates are and how they represent objects in 3D space, why ASL is NOT a manual version of English and has its own independent grammar, how Deaf culture and identity relate to ASL as a language vs. a communication tool) with streak XP, sign language science mastery badges (Handshape Expert, Spatial Grammar Scholar, Classifier Maestro, Deaf Culture Ambassador). Secondary ICP: parents of Deaf children, CODA adults, medical students, interpreters.

itunes_results.name: Brilliant
itunes_results.category: science and math learning app — visual interactive STEM problem solving; quantitative reasoning not a daily debate science education habit with streak XP, argumentation mastery badges (Modus Ponens Expert, Ad Hominem Detector, Burden of Proof Calibrator), and structured curriculum from formal logic to informal fallacy detection to competitive debate strategy

itunes_results.name: Simply Learn
itunes_results.category: tech certification prep app — IT and programming certification study; tech learning not a daily debate and critical argumentation habit with streak XP, logical reasoning mastery badges, and evidence-based curriculum from Aristotle's syllogistic logic to modern debate formats and Parliamentary procedure

itunes_results.name: The Debater
itunes_results.category: debate practice app — topic cards and argument prompts for informal practice; random topic generator not a daily debate science education habit with streak XP, formal argumentation mastery badges, and progression from opinion-haver to trained logical reasoner with systematic fallacy detection skills

daily_debate_skills_streak.notes: 3.89M debate/critical thinking community. Nobody gamifies the systematic science of argumentation. Gap: one debate science concept per day (what the Toulmin model is — claim, data, warrant, backing, qualifier, rebuttal — and how to map any argument onto it, how to identify the 20 most common logical fallacies including the subtle ones like 'modus tollens' vs. 'affirming the consequent', what Parliamentary debate format rules are and how British vs. American vs. Oxford debate formats differ, how to use the steel-man technique (present the strongest version of opponent's argument) before refuting, what burden of proof means and how it shifts during a debate exchange) with streak XP, argumentation mastery badges (Logician, Fallacy Hunter, Burden of Proof Expert, Steel-Man Architect), and progression from casual opinion-sharer to trained competitive debater. ICP: debate students, lawyers, consultants, policy analysts, anyone who argues for a living.

itunes_results.name: Culture Amp
itunes_results.category: HR employee engagement platform — workplace surveys and people analytics; HR enterprise tool not a daily cross-cultural communication science education habit with streak XP, cultural intelligence mastery badges (Hofstede Dimension Expert, High-Context/Low-Context Navigator, Cultural Intelligence Practitioner), and evidence-based curriculum from Hofstede's six cultural dimensions to the science of cultural intelligence (CQ)

itunes_results.name: HelloTalk
itunes_results.category: language exchange social app — chat with native speakers to practice language; language exchange partner tool not a daily cross-cultural communication science education habit with streak XP, cultural intelligence mastery badges, and structured curriculum from Edward Hall's monochronic vs. polychronic time cultures to Erin Meyer's Culture Map for business communication

itunes_results.name: Tandem
itunes_results.category: language exchange app — video calls with native speakers for language practice; conversation practice partner tool not a daily cross-cultural communication science education habit with streak XP, cultural intelligence mastery badges (Individualism-Collectivism Navigator, Power Distance Expert, Uncertainty Avoidance Scientist), and progression from cultural outsider to systematic cultural intelligence practitioner

daily_cross_cultural_communication_strea.notes: 995K cross-cultural community (plus the 450M+ people living or working across cultural borders). Language exchange apps practice the language but ignore the culture. Gap: one cross-cultural communication science concept per day (what Hofstede's six dimensions are — Power Distance, Individualism, Masculinity, Uncertainty Avoidance, Long-Term Orientation, Indulgence — and how they predict communication style, what Edward Hall's high-context vs. low-context communication means and why Japanese meetings feel indirect to Americans, how Erin Meyer's Culture Map explains why Dutch directness shocks American sensitivity, what cultural intelligence (CQ) is and why it predicts international business success better than IQ, how proxemics and touch norms vary across Mediterranean vs. Northern European vs. East Asian cultures) with streak XP, cultural intelligence mastery badges (Hofstede Scholar, High-Context Navigator, Cultural Intelligence Practitioner). ICP: expats, international students, global business professionals, HR practitioners — enormous underserved market.

itunes_results.name: Grammarly
itunes_results.category: grammar and writing assistant — real-time spelling, grammar, style suggestions; automated error correction tool not a daily prose science education habit with streak XP, writing craft mastery badges (Sentence Rhythm Engineer, Fog Index Optimizer, Clarity Scientist), and evidence-based curriculum from Flesch-Kincaid readability science to cognitive load theory applied to sentence structure

itunes_results.name: Hemingway Editor
itunes_results.category: writing clarity app — highlights complex sentences, passive voice, adverbs; clarity diagnostic not a daily prose science education habit with streak XP, writing style mastery badges (Active Voice Enforcer, Sentence Length Modulator, Readability Scientist), and structured curriculum from the neuroscience of reading comprehension to why Orwell's six rules of writing reflect actual cognitive processing limits

itunes_results.name: ProWritingAid
itunes_results.category: advanced writing analysis tool — style, grammar, pacing, repeated word detection; comprehensive editing tool not a daily prose science education habit with streak XP, prose craft mastery badges (Pacing Scientist, Fog Buster, Rhythm Architect), and progression from tool-dependent writer to independently skilled prose scientist who can diagnose their own writing without software

daily_prose_science_streak.notes: 4.25M writing community. Grammarly corrects errors; Hemingway flags complexity. Neither teaches the science of WHY certain prose works and other prose fails. Gap: one prose science concept per day (what Flesch-Kincaid readability science reveals about optimal sentence length — 14-17 words averages best comprehension, how the fog index measures abstraction burden, why Orwell's 'never use a long word where a short one will do' reflects cognitive load theory (working memory has 7±2 chunks), what the science of sentence rhythm is and how iambic stress patterns affect reader retention, how chunking and whitespace reduce cognitive load in persuasive writing) with streak XP, prose mastery badges (Readability Scientist, Cognitive Load Manager, Rhythm Engineer, Clarity Architect), and progression from grammar-correct to cognitively-optimized writer. Grammarly has 30M daily users — this is the science layer above what Grammarly does. ICP: content marketers, technical writers, bloggers, novelists, students.
